skill.name: next-best-practices
skill.description: Applies Next.js best practices for App Router architecture, React Server Components, data fetching, routing, metadata, assets, scripts, bundling, deployment, and debugging.
skill.summary: Guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to write and review modern Next.js applications.
skill.author: vercel-labs

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, network, script, environment, and filesystem patterns, but review found these are Markdown examples for Next.js guidance, not executable skill behavior. No prompt injection or malicious data exfiltration was found. One route-handler example may encourage returning a cookie token in an API response, so publication is acceptable with a security warning.

medium_findings.title: API Example Returns Cookie Token
medium_findings.description: The route handler request-helper example reads an authorization header and cookie token, then returns the token in a JSON response. This is documentation, not executable skill code, but it may lead generated code to expose authentication data if copied directly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly reads a cookie named token and includes it in Response.json. The risk is limited because the snippet is illustrative Markdown, not code run by the skill.

low_findings.title: Static Command And Script Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Most external command, dynamic import, script, and network detections are code fences or inline examples explaining normal Next.js usage. They do not execute during skill loading and do not instruct the assistant to run hidden commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative locations are Markdown documentation snippets. No executable scripts, hooks, install commands, or hidden runtime behavior were found in the reviewed skill files.

low_findings.title: Environment And Filesystem Mentions Are Deployment Guidance
low_findings.description: Environment variables, .next paths, Redis, S3, and Docker examples appear in self-hosting guidance. These examples describe deployment setup and do not access local secrets from the evaluator or marketplace runtime.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is in fenced deployment examples with placeholder values. There is no code path that reads environment files or transmits secrets.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sensitive Token Echo In Example Response
dangerous_patterns.description: A request-helper example demonstrates returning a cookie token to the client. This pattern should be revised before users copy it into production code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The snippet directly returns a token value. The danger is instructional rather than an active malicious payload.

dangerous_patterns.title: Development MCP Debug Endpoint Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents local Next.js MCP debug requests that can reveal routes, project metadata, logs, and server action identifiers. This is legitimate development guidance, but users should keep it limited to local trusted development servers.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The MCP examples intentionally call local debugging tools. They are legitimate, but exposing the dev endpoint beyond localhost could disclose project metadata.

content.value_statement: Next.js projects often fail from unclear boundaries, slow data fetching, and deployment mistakes. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused rules for modern App Router work.
actual_capabilities: Reviews App Router file conventions, route segments, parallel routes, and special files.
Guides React Server Component boundaries, client components, directives, and serializable props.
Recommends data fetching patterns for Server Components, Server Actions, Route Handlers, and Suspense.
Explains metadata, Open Graph images, image optimization, font loading, and script loading patterns.
Provides self-hosting guidance for standalone output, Docker, cache handlers, and health checks.
Helps debug hydration errors, bundling issues, async API migrations, and selected build routes.
limitations: It is a documentation skill and does not run Next.js diagnostics by itself.
It may not cover every new Next.js release or experimental option immediately.
Security-sensitive snippets still need project-specific review before production use.
It assumes the App Router and modern Next.js patterns unless the user provides different context.

use_cases.title: Review an App Router migration
use_cases.description: Check pages, layouts, async params, metadata, and route handlers against current Next.js conventions.

use_cases.title: Plan production deployment
use_cases.description: Choose standalone output, Docker settings, cache handlers, image strategy, and health checks for self-hosting.

use_cases.title: Debug performance and rendering issues
use_cases.description: Diagnose hydration errors, data waterfalls, bundle problems, script loading, and font or image configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one route or component.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Next.js page for App Router best practices. Focus on file conventions, server and client boundaries, and obvious data fetching issues.

prompt_templates.title: Fix a Data Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve a slow or confusing data fetching path.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Next.js data flow. Recommend whether to use a Server Component, Server Action, Route Handler, Promise.all, Suspense, or preload pattern.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Production
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Next.js app for production deployment. Check image and font optimization, scripts, bundling, standalone output, cache behavior, and health checks.

prompt_templates.title: Modernize an App
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced migration across several framework areas.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a migration plan for this Next.js app. Cover async request APIs, React Server Component boundaries, metadata, route handlers, Turbopack bundling, and self-hosting risks.

output_examples.input: Review a route that fetches user data, posts, and comments sequentially.
output: Identifies the data waterfall and recommends parallel fetching or Suspense boundaries.
Explains which work belongs in Server Components and which belongs in Server Actions.
Calls out any client component boundary that forces unnecessary browser-side data fetching.

output_examples.input: Prepare a Next.js app for Docker self-hosting.
output: Recommends standalone output, static asset copying, non-root runtime users, and health checks.
Flags ISR cache behavior that needs shared storage in multi-instance deployments.
Lists environment variables that must stay server-side and should not use NEXT_PUBLIC prefixes.

output_examples.input: Investigate hydration errors after adding a browser-only package.
output: Separates browser-only code from Server Components.
Suggests a client wrapper or dynamic loading when server rendering is not safe.
Checks related script, CSS, and package bundling choices.

best_practices: Provide the relevant files, framework version, and router type before asking for a review.
Ask for security review when examples touch cookies, headers, tokens, or environment variables.
Verify suggested changes with build, lint, type checks, and route-specific runtime testing.
anti_patterns: Do not apply every recommendation blindly to Pages Router projects or older Next.js versions.
Do not expose server-only environment values through public config endpoints.
Do not copy illustrative auth or token snippets without adapting them to your security model.

faq.question: Does this skill support the Next.js App Router?
faq.answer: Yes. Most guidance targets App Router conventions, React Server Components, Server Actions, and Route Handlers.

faq.question: Can it update my code automatically?
faq.answer: It can guide Claude, Codex, or Claude Code while they edit code, but the skill itself is documentation.

faq.question: Does it replace the official Next.js documentation?
faq.answer: No. It summarizes practical patterns, but official documentation should be checked for version-specific changes.

faq.question: Is it useful for self-hosted deployments?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers standalone output, Docker, cache handlers, environment variables, image optimization, and health checks.

faq.question: Does it handle security review?
faq.answer: It includes some secure boundaries, but dedicated review is still needed for authentication, secrets, and public APIs.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
